data.title: Super Micro Computer sinks even as CEO 'confident' stock will not be delisted
data.description:
• Super Micro Computer (NASDAQ:SMCI) shares fell more than 8% on Tuesday, even as CEO Charles Liang told Reuters he was "confident" shares of the artificial intelligence server maker will not be delisted from the Nasdaq.
  • On Friday, Super Micro received a letter from the Nasdaq informing the company that the exchange granted its request for an extension. The extension gives the company until February 25, 2025, to file its fiscal 2024 Annual Report on Form 10-K, its Quarterly Report on Form 10-Q for the period ended Sept. 30, 2024 and any other required filings.
  • As such, common stock in Super Micro will remain listed on the exchange during the exception period. The company currently expects to file all the required reports by February 25, 2025.
  • Earlier this month, an independent special committee appointed by Super Micro said it found no evidence of misconduct by the company's board of directors or audit committee. However, as part of the investigating committee’s recommendations, Super Micro will appoint a new CFO, chief compliance officer and general counsel. The company named Kenneth Cheung, previously the vice president of finance, as its new chief accounting officer.

(Seeking Alpha's Tiyashi Datta and Preeti Singh contributed to this story.)

data.title: Union chief predicts US Steel's long-term decline if Japanese take over
data.description:
US Steel (NYSE:X) -2.5% in Tuesday's trading as United Steelworkers union head David McCall said Tuesday he has not received assurances that Nippon Steel (OTCPK:NPSCY) (OTCPK:NISTF) is committed to ensuring the lasting success of the American firm in its proposed takeover.

One of McCall's biggest concerns is that Nippon (OTCPK:NPSCY) (OTCPK:NISTF) may import steel into the U.S. from its international mills, a move he said would erode the iconic U.S. company.

Nippon Steel (OTCPK:NPSCY) (OTCPK:NISTF) wants a return on its investment, "and I understand that but it can't be harvesting our facilities and letting them slowly but surely over a period of time deteriorate so that they can then bring product in from their other facilities around the world and have access to our market," McCall told Reuters in an interview.

The Japanese company has denied it would import steel from its international mills into U.S. and said it aims to become an "insider" in the U.S., a market it considers crucial to its growth plans.

McCall also criticized Nippon Steel (OTCPK:NPSCY) (OTCPK:NISTF) for not trying to stop or intervene in "bullying" from US Steel (X) CEO David Burritt to close the deal.

Nippon Steel (OTCPK:NPSCY) (OTCPK:NISTF) unveiled details of the various commitments it has made to win union support, including on job security and investments in facilities.

data.title: Matthews International edges higher as activist pushes for CEO exit, board seats
data.description:
Matthews International (MATW) ticked higher by 0.4% as activist holder Barington Capital pushed for the casket-maker to replace its CEO, sell off underperforming business and nominate three board directors.

Barington Capital, which owns a ~2% stake in Matthews (NASDAQ:MATW), wants the company to start a search for a new CEO to replace Joe Bartolacci, according to a letter the company sent to Matthews board on Tuesday.

"We are patient, long-term investors, but we can no longer accept the status quo," Barington's James Mitarotonda wrote in the letter. "We strongly believe that Matthews needs to take decisive steps to address its prolonged period of share price underperformance and unlock its value potential."

Barington has been an investor in Matthews since 2022 and the hedge fund pushed for a refreshed board in December of that year.

"Based on the Company’s performance during Mr. Bartolacci’s extended 18-year tenure, we do not believe that he is the right leader to unlock Matthews’ significant value potential," Mitarotonda added.

Barington is calling for Matthew's to explore the sale of its "poorly performing" packaging business, SGK Brand Solutions.

data.title: Cardiff stock soars on onvansertib data, $40M direct offering
data.description:
Cardiff Oncology (NASDAQ:CRDF) stock rallied 46% Tuesday after it reported positive data for its oncology drug onvansertib and priced a $40M registered direct offering.

Cardiff (CRDF) said a Phase 2 study of onvansertib in the treatment of RAS-mutated metastatic colorectal cancer showed a 64% objective response rate for patients given a 30mg dose of the drug in addition to standard of care, versus 33% for standard of care alone, according to a statement.

The study also showed a 50% objective response rate at the 20mg dose of the drug versus standard of care alone.

Cardiff (CRDF) also announced the pricing of a $40M underwritten registered direct offering early Tuesday.

data.title: Ceres Global Ag announces new CEO
data.description:
• Ceres Global Ag (CRP:CA) announced that Carlos Paz has resigned as the president and chief executive officer and a director of the corporation effective December 31, 2024.
  • The board has appointed Tom Coyle as interim president and CEO, effective January 1, 2025.
  • Coyle serves as an advisor to the board.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Pinterest dips, Alphabet and Vimeo gain amid Piper Sandler internet update
data.description:
Pinterest (NYSE:PINS) and IAC (IAC) were dipping Tuesday morning, and Vimeo (NASDAQ:VMEO) surging, alongside ratings changes from Piper Sandler in an update on internet stocks.

Meanwhile, Alphabet (NASDAQ:GOOG) (NASDAQ:GOOGL) was notably higher as the firm reiterated its Overweight rating and named it its top mega-cap pick.

Pinterest (PINS) was off 4% as analyst Thomas Champion downgraded the stock to Neutral: "We move to the sidelines with this name following 2 quarters of mixed prints."

After guidance for 15-17% growth in Q4, the company is unlikely to hit 20% growth for the full year, he noted, and it looks like low double-digit growth for Q1. "Our early read on Performance+ is mixed and our Ad Buyer Survey suggests a competitive SMID advertising field," he added. Champion cut Pinterest's (PINS) price target to $36.

As for IAC, down 2%, "the current asset configuration is not compelling," he said, tweaking estimates down and downgrading to Neutral as he awaits the implications of spinning off Angi (ANGI).

Vimeo (VMEO) was 10% higher as the firm upgraded to Overweight and named it its top small-cap pick. "Management has spent the last two years resetting the cost structure. Low calorie marketing spend has been weeded out. At the same time, the Enterprise business has flourished and now at $100M-plus annual run rate, which will likely return the business to growth regardless of the outcome in Self-Serve," Champion wrote.

As for Alphabet -- (GOOG) +3.8%, (GOOGL) +3.9% -- "Our recent surveys suggest GOOGL is headed for a product-driven resurgence," Champion wrote. "Among ad buyers, 75% said AI tools were the most disruptive into next year and Gemini & PMax highlighted. Among CIOs, GCP was named the 'most strategic' AI vendor."

data.title: Sirius XM stock falls as 2025 revenue outlook falls short of expectations
data.description:
Sirius XM (NASDAQ:SIRI) stock fell 8.5% after the company's 2025 revenue outlook fell short of expectations.

The firm said it expects 2025 revenue of $8.5B, below the Wall Street consensus of $8.74B.

The company expects adjusted core profit of $2.6B and free cash flow of $1.15B in 2025.

"Aligned with its enhanced focus on free cash flow, the company expects free cash flow conversion, free cash flow as a percentage of core profit, to increase from ~ 37% in 2024 to approximately 44% in 2025," the firm said.

The firm also said it is targeting free cash flow of $1.5B in 2027.

The company also expects to reduce its debt by ~$700M in 2025 and achieve a leverage ratio of 3.6x by year end 2025, and added it is committed to its current quarterly dividend of $0.27 per share, or $1.08 per share annually.

Separately, the firm said it has appointed Wayne Thorsen as its COO and executive vice president, effective December 16.

Thorsen most recently served as the chief business officer at ADT.

In the newly-created role of COO at SIRI, Thorsen will oversee its product and technology functions and be responsible for aspects of the company's commercial activities, business development, and corporate strategy.

The firm also said Joseph Inzerillo has stepped down from his role as chief product and technology officer, effective immediately.

data.title: Citizens Financial says private bank unit to be profitable in Q4
data.description:
Citizens Financial Group (NYSE:CFG) said its private bank unit will be profitable in Q4 2024 after breaking even in Q3, according to slides for a presentation at an industry conference on Tuesday. It expects to contribute ~5% of the company's total earnings in 2025.

The bank has been expanding its private bank presence in regions such as San Francisco, San Diego, and Newport Beach, California, as well as Boston and New York City.

Citizens (NYSE:CFG) is well positioned to reach ~16-18% return on tangible common equity over the medium term given its strategic initiatives and 2025 tailwinds, management said.

CFG stock jumped 4.2% in late morning trading on Tuesday.

The company sees significant net interest income tailwinds from non-core and swaps over the medium term, targeting a net interest margin range of 3.25%-3.40%.

Private Bank results should move from a net investment position toward 20%+ ROTCE over the medium term.

It expects the current significant drag from non-corer businesses to dissipate over time.

data.title: Auto 2025: These stocks may outperform despite industry speed bumps
data.description:
Morgan Stanley released its 2025 preview of the automobile sector on Tuesday. The firm stayed true to the consensus view that the year ahead will be a bumpy road for investors due to the impact of new EV policies, push for onshoring issues, tariff risk, and uncertain inflation rates.

Cutting against the grain a bit, analyst Adam Jonas said the U.S. election result has extended the 'ICE is Nice' trade for a bit longer, but he said to keep on the lookout for hidden value in the EV ecosystem into the second half. As for the overall U.S. auto market, the expectations are for 3.2% year-over-year growth in 2025 and a lower battery-electric penetration rate of 8.5%.

He said 2025 is shaping up to be a volatile year, dominated by expected U.S. relaxation of emission standards and the potential for sharply increased tariffs. Jonas noted that since the U.S. Presidential election, investors have been bidding up ICE-exposed names such as General Motors (NYSE:GM) and Phinia (PHIN) relative to EV-oriented names like Rivian Automotive (NASDAQ:RIVN) and Aptiv (APTV).

Stock picks

Morgan Stanley named Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) as its top auto stock for 2025. The company's potential to play a greater role in the U.S. renewable/autonomous industrial complex is seen as a tailwind. Jonas and his team assigned a base case price target of $400 to Tesla (TSLA) and a bull case price target of $600.

Lucid Group (LCID) is still rated at Underweight heading into the new year. Meanwhile, Morgan Stanley boosted General Motors (GM) to an Equal-weight rating to join Ford Motor (NYSE:F). "Curtailment of EV incentives may extend the de-adoption of profitable ICE vehicles which can support FCF for the US-based legacy OEMs," highlighted Jonas.

Heading into 2025, Morgan Stanley reiterated its preference for the franchise dealer complex, which was said to be in the'latter innings' of gross profit per unit mean reversion relative to the legacy OEMs. Top picks from MS on the retail side of the auto business are Group 1 Automotive (GPI), Penske Automotive Group (PAG), and AutoNation (AN).

As for suppliers, Morgan Stanley selected BorgWarner (BWA), (AXL), and Lear (LEA) as its top picks to hold into 2025.

A check of Seeking Alpha's Quant Rating system shows Carvana (CVNA), Group 1 Automotive (GPI), General Motors (GM), XPeng (XPEV), BYD Company Limited (OTCPK:BYDDF), Strattec Security (STRT), Dorman Products (DORM), and Cooper-Standard (CPS) as buy-rated based purely on quantitative analysis.

data.title: Jones Lang LaSalle raised to Outperform as KBW sees 'significant' margin expansion
data.description:
Keefe, Bruyette & Woods upgraded Jones Lang LaSalle (NYSE:JLL) stock to Outperform from Market Perform in a recent note as analyst Jade Rahmani sees "significant" margin expansion over the next couple of years, driven by a recovery in commercial real estate.

The commercial property and investment management company is expected to benefit from a CRE recovery, "given its over 50% revenue mix of leasing/capital markets," Rahmani noted.

"Positive factors are in place for CRE recovery to take hold and continue into 2025 including prices and transaction volumes having bottomed, down over 20% and over 60% from the peak, lender reserves having increased over the past 2 years, the outlook for economic growth solid and potentially poised to increase, and investor sentiment toward CRE having improved with range-bound expectations regarding interest rates," the note, released Monday, said.

KBW's Outperform rate compares with the SA Quant system rating of Hold and the average Wall Street analyst rating of Buy.

(JLL) shares edged up 0.3% in Tuesday morning trading.
